title: Plan structured parsing pipeline for Pleno PDF data extraction
description: Design the next feature after PDF discovery/download: extract structured parliamentary data from the downloaded Pleno PDFs without relying on OCR alone. The plan should define the target schema, a hybrid extraction stack (native PDF text/tables + OCR fallback + model-based normalization), candidate managed services such as OpenAI Responses API PDF inputs and AWS Textract, evaluation criteria, tests, and rollout sequence.
close_reason: Plan documented in /plans and commit grouping proposed

title: Default HTML cache prevents sync-pleno from discovering new upstream sessions
description: discover_pleno reuses public_page.html and expanded_view.html whenever they exist and refresh_html is false. Because discover-pleno and sync-pleno default to refresh_html=false, routine runs can stay pinned to stale local snapshots indefinitely and never observe newly published Pleno sessions unless the operator remembers to force --refresh-html. Either invert the default for sync, add TTL/staleness checks, or make cached mode an explicit offline/sampling mode.

title: Add manifest persistence regression coverage and operator guidance
description:
Title: Add manifest persistence regression coverage and operator guidance
Owner: Adrian Alarcon
Status: todo
Goal: Lock in the corruption contract and atomic persistence behavior with focused tests and runbook updates.
Scope: Add tests for malformed JSONL input and interrupted or failed persistence commits., Verify service flows preserve canonical manifest state after failure scenarios., Update operator documentation for corruption detection and recovery.
Files: tests/test_manifest.py, tests/test_services.py, docs/runbook-pleno-cli.md
Dependencies: congreso-votaciones-445, congreso-votaciones-445.2
Constraints: Keep coverage fixture-backed and offline-reproducible; no live Congreso access is required.
Plan: Add regression coverage after the loader and writer contracts are implemented so tests encode the final behavior.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Automated tests cover malformed manifest input with the documented error contract.
- Automated tests cover at least one interrupted or failed write path without leaving partial canonical state behind.
- The runbook explains how operators detect, recover from, and re-run after manifest corruption or persistence failure.
Contracts: Tests and docs must reflect JSONL as the canonical manifest and CSV as a derived export.
Code: Extend tests around load_manifest, _persist_manifest callers, and failure-path service behavior.
Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_manifest.py tests/test_services.py
Docs: docs/runbook-pleno-cli.md, docs/specs/perform-an-extensive-code-review-20260321T010428Z-d6b4001d.md
Rollout/Rollback: This verification task should complete before closing congreso-votaciones-445 or starting the dependent download-metadata work.
Observability: Document the expected operator-visible error text and recovery steps.
Notes: This task closes the loop between persistence semantics, user-facing guidance, and fixture-backed evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- Automated tests cover malformed manifest input with the documented error contract.
- Automated tests cover at least one interrupted or failed write path without leaving partial canonical state behind.
- The runbook explains how operators detect, recover from, and re-run after manifest corruption or persistence failure.

title: Implement atomic manifest writes for JSONL and CSV
description:
Title: Implement atomic manifest writes for JSONL and CSV
Owner: Adrian Alarcon
Status: todo
Goal: Replace direct manifest writes with temp-file-plus-replace persistence that preserves canonical files across interruptions.
Scope: Add same-directory temp-file write helpers for JSONL and CSV outputs., Update service persistence sequencing to align with the canonical-manifest contract., Ensure failed writes do not leave partial canonical files behind.
Files: src/congreso_votaciones/manifest.py, src/congreso_votaciones/services.py
Dependencies: congreso-votaciones-445, congreso-votaciones-445.1
Constraints: Preserve current CLI-facing paths and keep JSONL as the canonical runtime input.
Plan: Stage both artifacts through temp files, then replace canonical outputs in a sequence that favors canonical JSONL correctness.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Manifest JSONL and CSV writes use temp-file-plus-replace semantics in the target directory.
- A write failure does not leave a truncated canonical manifest file at either persisted path.
- Service-level persistence sequencing follows the documented canonical-manifest contract.
Contracts: JSONL remains the source of truth loaded by runtime commands; CSV is a derived export that should not corrupt JSONL state.
Code: Refactor write_manifest_csv, write_manifest_jsonl, and _persist_manifest around shared atomic persistence helpers.
Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_manifest.py tests/test_services.py
Docs: docs/specs/perform-an-extensive-code-review-20260321T010428Z-d6b4001d.md
Rollout/Rollback: Land after the loader contract is explicit so persistence ordering matches the documented recovery model.
Observability: Persistence failures should be attributable to the specific target path that could not be committed.
Notes: This task should minimize drift between CSV and JSONL while prioritizing correctness of the canonical JSONL manifest.
acceptance_criteria:
- Manifest JSONL and CSV writes use temp-file-plus-replace semantics in the target directory.
- A write failure does not leave a truncated canonical manifest file at either persisted path.
- Service-level persistence sequencing follows the documented canonical-manifest contract.

title: Codify malformed manifest contract and recovery path
description:
Title: Codify malformed manifest contract and recovery path
Owner: Adrian Alarcon
Status: todo
Goal: Make malformed manifest input fail deterministically with operator-facing recovery guidance before atomic persistence changes land.
Scope: Define the canonical persisted source of truth between JSONL and CSV., Surface malformed JSONL parse or validation failures with path and line context., Document the operator recovery path for corrupted manifest state.
Files: src/congreso_votaciones/manifest.py, src/congreso_votaciones/services.py, docs/runbook-pleno-cli.md
Dependencies: congreso-votaciones-445
Constraints: Assume JSONL remains the canonical manifest because runtime loads only manifest_jsonl_path today.
Plan: Define the fail-fast contract first so downstream persistence and download work share one recovery model.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Malformed manifest input fails fast instead of being silently skipped or partially loaded.
- The surfaced error identifies the manifest path and the failing line or validation context with an actionable recovery hint.
- The runbook states which manifest artifact is canonical and what operators should do when corruption is detected.
Contracts: JSONL is the canonical persisted manifest; CSV remains a derived export.
Code: Introduce a deterministic loader error surface for malformed JSONL or invalid ManifestRecord payloads.
Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_manifest.py tests/test_services.py
Docs: docs/runbook-pleno-cli.md, docs/specs/perform-an-extensive-code-review-20260321T010428Z-d6b4001d.md
Rollout/Rollback: Land before atomic persistence so later changes can target one documented contract.
Observability: Loader and service failures should mention the corrupted path without requiring debug mode.
Notes: This task narrows the persistence contract so partial-write handling has a clear canonical file target.
acceptance_criteria:
- Malformed manifest input fails fast instead of being silently skipped or partially loaded.
- The surfaced error identifies the manifest path and the failing line or validation context with an actionable recovery hint.
- The runbook states which manifest artifact is canonical and what operators should do when corruption is detected.

title: Fix invalid autocodex.yaml so harness preflight can run
description: autocodex harness preflight --strict currently fails before any review/test logic because autocodex.yaml defines the top-level autonomy key twice. The duplicate key at the end of the file makes the YAML invalid for the harness, so the repo cannot execute the configured preflight gate that the same config declares mandatory. Remove the duplicate block and re-verify harness preflight plus lint.

title: Clarify or normalize missing parliamentary metadata in parsed records

Title: Clarify or normalize missing parliamentary metadata in parsed records
